name: Heartland Corn Chowder
recipeIngredient: 1 Green bell pepper, seeded and sliced
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the smoked ham bones, onions, carrots, potatoes, celery, green bell pepper, bay leaves, thyme, salt, and pepper in a large pot filled with water. Bring to a boil over medium-high heat.
2. Reduce heat to maintain a slow boil for 2 1/2 to 3 hours, or until the vegetables are tender.
3. Strain the broth through a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl, discarding the bay leaves and bones.
4. Skim off any fat from the surface of the broth.
5. Transfer the cooked vegetables and ham to a food processor and puree until smooth.
6. Return the pureed mixture to the pot and bring the broth back to a boil.
7. Stir in the milk and corn kernels, then simmer for 5 minutes.
8. Ladle the chowder into bowls and garnish with bacon slices.
9. Serve immediately. Makes 6 to 8 servings.
